title: Tectaria ridleyana
intro: Tectaria ridleyana, also known as Indian fan fern or Asian fan fern, is a type of fern plant that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Tectaria ridleyana include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Tectaria ridleyana is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used to treat respiratory problems, and also in the form of capsules or tablets for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
taxonomy: Tectaria ridleyana is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Tectaria and the order Polypodiales. The family Dryopteridaceae is a group of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns or shield ferns. The genus Tectaria is a group of ferns characterized by their simple or pinnate leaves. Tectaria ridleyana is a tropical species native to certain parts of the world.
